question: Blues vs. Sharks: O/U 5.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 30 at 10:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Blues and Sharks combine to score 6 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 6, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

events.title: Blues vs. Sharks
events.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 30 at 10:00PM ET:
If the Blues win, the market will resolve to "Blues".
If the Sharks win, the market will resolve to "Sharks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis Blues hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their upcoming matchup against the San Jose Sharks, with implied probabilities reflecting the Blues' stronger early-season form at 2-1-0 compared to the Sharks' 0-2-1 record amid defensive struggles. Recent injury reports confirm Blues forward Dylan Holloway is day-to-day but expected to play, bolstering their top-six depth, while Sharks goaltender Vitek Vanecek remains sidelined, forcing reliance on less proven options. Home-ice advantage at Enterprise Center favors St. Louis, where they've posted a solid 60% win rate historically versus San Jose's road woes. Momentum from the Blues' recent overtime win adds rest edge, though the Sharks' youthful offense could spark an upset in this low-scoring affair.

question: Will Stephen Lynch be the Democratic nominee for MA-08?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the MA-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on September 1, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: MA-08 Democratic Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the MA-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on September 1, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Incumbent advantage positions Rep. Stephen Lynch as trader favorite at 51% implied probability** to win Massachusetts' 8th Congressional District Democratic primary on September 3. His established fundraising edge—over $1 million raised—and endorsements from labor unions and local Democrats underpin this lead, contrasting challengers Patrick Roath (39%) and Andrew Zylberfink (35.8%), who draw progressive support criticizing Lynch's centrist record on housing and Israel policy. Recent catalysts include a mid-August Emerson poll showing Lynch ahead 47-31-22, Roath's grassroots surge via viral social media on affordability issues, and Zylberfink's self-funded tech appeal, tightening the multi-candidate field amid high early voting turnout. Traders price in incumbents' 90%+ historical primary win rate in safe blue districts, though base turnout could shift odds.

question: Will Edouard Philippe be the next mayor of Le Havre?
description:
Municipal elections to elect the municipal council of Le Havre are scheduled to take place on March 15, 2026 (with a runoff on March 22 if necessary); the elected councillors will then choose the mayor.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next elected Mayor of Le Havre.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Edouard Philippe's near-certain frontrunner status in the Le Havre mayoral election stems from his strong incumbency advantage as the sitting mayor, having secured 65% in the 2020 municipal vote amid a fragmented opposition. Recent trader consensus on Polymarket reflects his enduring local popularity, bolstered by infrastructure projects and economic stability claims, with no credible challengers yet emerging from parties like Les Républicains or the National Rally. Challengers such as Charlotte Boulogne or Jean-Paul Lecoq trail far in implied probabilities due to limited visibility. Realistic shifts could arise from a high-profile scandal, national political turbulence affecting Horizons party support, or a unified right-wing coalition by 2026, though current evidence points to continuity.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next elected Mayor of Toulon.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder mayors appointed before the election will not be considered.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Josée Massi's commanding 96.5% implied probability in the Toulon mayoral election stems primarily from her designation as successor by longtime mayor Hubert Falco, whose decades-long dominance with Les Républicains gives her strong incumbency advantages, organizational backing, and voter familiarity ahead of the 2026 municipal vote. Recent polls show her leading the first round by wide margins, bolstered by departmental vice-presidency experience and local endorsements, reflecting trader consensus on her path to victory in a likely runoff. Realistic challenges include a National Rally surge led by Laure Lavalette amid national far-right momentum, an unforeseen scandal eroding Falco's legacy support, or fragmented left-wing consolidation, though current evidence suggests low upset risk.

question: Kurds declare independence from Iran?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if PJAK (Party for a Free Life in Kurdistan), the Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan (PDKI), Komala, the Kurdistan Freedom Party (PAK), or a successor Kurdish organization with broad recognition among Iranian Kurdish opposition groups, or a Kurdish official within Iran formally declares the creation of a new independent state separate from Iran and asserts governing authority over a specified territory within Iran before June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying public announcement alone is sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether governing authority is actually established, maintained, or recognized.

A declaration must include a clear public statement by the organization or its leadership announcing the establishment of an independent political entity separate from Iran and claiming governmental authority over an identified geographic area previously under Iranian governance.

The claimed territory does not need to be precisely defined but must be at least partially specified. A majority of the claimed territory must lie within the internationally recognized borders of Iran as they existed at the time of this market’s creation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a mere 17% chance of Kurds declaring independence from Iran, driven by Tehran's ironclad control over northwestern provinces like Kurdistan and West Azerbaijan, where separatist groups face severe crackdowns. No major Kurdish factions, such as the KDPI or PJAK, have issued formal independence proclamations recently, prioritizing survival amid ongoing military operations. Recent news highlights sporadic clashes and protests tied to broader Iranian unrest, but lacks evidence of coordinated secessionist momentum or international backing, reinforcing the "No" outcome as baseline expectation absent seismic shifts like regime collapse.

question: Will the New York Yankees win more than 86.5 games in the 2026 MLB Regular Season?
description:
This market will resolve to "O 86.5" if the New York Yankees win more than 86.5 games during the 2026 MLB Regular Season.

If at any point it becomes statistically impossible for the New York Yankees to record more than 86.5 wins per the rules of the MLB, the corresponding market will resolve to "U 86.5".

If the MLB Regular Season is cancelled, partially completed, or postponed after November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "50-50".

The resolution source will be official information from Major League Baseball (https://www.mlb.com/standings/); however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: MLB: 2026 Regular Season Win Totals
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the final regular season record of the listed team during the 2026 MLB Regular Season. If the listed team records more than the listed number of wins, the corresponding market will to its corresponding “Over”. Otherwise, it will resolve to its corresponding “Under”.

If at any point it becomes statistically impossible for the listed team to record more than listed number of wins per the rules of the MLB, the corresponding market will resolve to corresponding "Under".

If the MLB Regular Season is cancelled, partially completed, or postponed after November 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, these markets will resolve to "50-50".

The resolution source will be official information from Major League Baseball (https://www.mlb.com/standings/); however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on MLB 2026 regular season win totals prices juggernauts like the Dodgers around 102.5 wins (65% over probability) due to their stacked lineup, elite rotation additions, and 2025 postseason momentum, while rebuilding Athletics languish near 62.5 (80% under). Recent offseason fireworks—Dodgers signing Roki Sasaki and bolstering bullpen depth, Yankees retaining Juan Soto long-term—have lifted their lines, contrasted by Nationals' farm system graduations pushing modest overs. Upcoming winter meetings, arbitration rulings, and injury recoveries from 2025 slumps introduce volatility, as free agents like Corbin Burnes could reshape mid-tier contenders like Phillies or Braves, highlighting the wisdom of crowds in early projections.

question: Will Valve add Cache to the Map Pool by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Valve adds the map Cache to the official map pool by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If Cache is added to the Active Duty pool, it must remain there continuously for at least 48 hours for this market to resolve to “Yes”. Temporary additions (e.g., for testing) that are reversed within 48 hours will not count. If Cache is added after the deadline, this market will resolve to “No”, even if the addition was announced earlier.

For the purpose of this market, “Valve” refers to Valve Corporation, the developer and publisher of the Counter-Strike series.

For the purpose of this market, the “official map pool” refers to the Active Duty map group in Counter-Strike 2 (CS2), which consists of the maps eligible for both competitive matchmaking and professional tournaments.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Valve; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Valve has issued no official announcement adding Cache to the CS2 Active Duty map pool, anchoring trader consensus around a "No" outcome as Shanghai Major concluded without changes. The map, absent since its 2020 CS:GO removal, topped recent pro player polls at events like BLAST Austin qualifiers, fueling community campaigns and tweets from stars like s1mple urging its return for balanced bomb sites and CT-side appeal. However, Valve's rotations prioritize stability around majors, with the next Perfect World event looming in early 2025 potentially prompting tweaks—though historical patterns show infrequent revivals amid pool saturation at eight maps. Odds reflect this uncertainty, blending pro sentiment against Valve's deliberate pace.

question: US announces military support of Kurds in Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government officially announces or confirms the United States is engaging in military coordination with, or providing military support to, Kurdish groups operating in Iran by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For announcements to qualify, they must be official policy announcements or definitive statements by the US President.

For confirmations to qualify, they must be unambiguous, on-the-record public statements issued by an authorized governmental person or entity, speaking in an official capacity.

Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement or qualifying confirmation will not be considered.
 
For the purposes of this market, military coordination or support includes but is not limited to joint operations, direct battlefield assistance, intelligence sharing, weapons or equipment transfers, training, logistics support, or other clearly stated military assistance.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 95.8% implied probability, driven by the complete absence of any official US statements, Defense Department briefings, or White House announcements signaling military support for Iran's Kurdish minorities by March 31. US policy has long prioritized economic sanctions, diplomatic isolation, and indirect pressure on Tehran over direct backing of internal dissidents, avoiding escalation risks amid broader Middle East tensions like Israel-Iran exchanges and Syria operations. No recent catalysts—such as congressional resolutions, Pentagon aid packages, or envoy meetings—have emerged to shift sentiment. Realistic wildcards include sudden regime crackdowns sparking mass unrest or a major Iranian provocation prompting policy reversal, though evidence points to continuity in restrained US-Iran posture.

events.title: Estoril Praia vs. Rio Ave FC

In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 22, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: SC Braga vs. FC Porto
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for Sunday, March 22, 2026 between SC Braga and FC Porto.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Porto's superior league position and recent form—unbeaten in five Primeira Liga matches with three clean sheets—drive their 46% implied probability as trader favorites against SC Braga, despite the hosts' solid home record. Braga's 28% share reflects their attacking momentum, including a 3-1 win over Rio Ave, but defensive vulnerabilities exposed in a midweek cup loss temper expectations. The draw at 28% gains traction from five stalemates in the last eight head-to-heads, bolstered by Porto's key absences (Pepe doubtful, Taremi sold) and Braga's rest advantage post-international break. Official lineups pending, but crowd wisdom prices Porto's depth as the edge in this rivalry clash.

question: Will Russia enter Dovha Balka by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Dovha Balka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.48905979884506° N, 37.60464316125542° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have made no confirmed entry into Dovha Balka, a frontline settlement in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia region, maintaining a distance of several kilometers amid stalled advances. Recent Ukrainian military updates and OSINT monitoring report repelled assaults near nearby Robotyne and Verbove, bolstered by minefields, drone strikes, and harsh spring terrain limiting mechanized pushes. Official Russian channels claim incremental gains elsewhere but none targeting Dovha Balka specifically. With the March 31 deadline approaching and historical advance rates averaging mere hundreds of meters daily, traders price a 94% "No" probability, reflecting consensus on resilient Ukrainian defenses sustaining the status quo absent major breakthroughs.

question: National T20 Cup: Karachi Region Whites vs Tbc A
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Karachi Region Whites and Tbc A scheduled for March 7 2026 in National T20 Cup.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: National T20 Cup: Karachi Region Whites vs Tbc A - Toss Match Double Karachi Region Whites Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Karachi Region Whites and Tbc A scheduled for 2026-03-07 in National T20 Cup.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Karachi Region Whites will be considered correct if  Karachi Region Whites is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Tbc A will be considered correct if Tbc A is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: National T20 Cup: Karachi Region Whites vs Tbc A - Most Sixes Karachi Region Whites Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Karachi Region Whites and Tbc A scheduled for 2026-03-07 in National T20 Cup.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Karachi Region Whites will be considered correct if Karachi Region Whites is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Tbc A.The outcome corresponding to Tbc A will be considered correct if Tbc A is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Karachi Region Whites.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Karachi Region Whites and Tbc A scheduled for 2026-03-07 in National T20 Cup.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Karachi Region Whites will be considered correct if Karachi Region Whites is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Tbc A.The outcome corresponding to Tbc A will be considered correct if Tbc A is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Karachi Region Whites.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Karachi Region Whites and TBC A enter their National T20 Cup clash with evenly matched batting firepower, fueling the tight trader consensus around a potential draw at 52%, with KAR and TBC A hovering near 51-52%. Both sides boast aggressive top-order hitters capable of clearing ropes—Karachi relying on recent form from powerplay explosives like their openers' 15+ sixes across last three outings, while TBC A counters with middle-order sluggers who posted match-high totals in prior games. Neutral venue conditions favor high-scoring affairs without clear rest or momentum edges, and no confirmed injuries tilt probabilities, reflecting crowd wisdom in this unpredictable sixes race where small margins decide dominance.

question: National T20 Cup: Karachi Region Whites vs Tbc A - Team Top Batter Karachi Region Whites Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Karachi Region Whites and Tbc A scheduled for 2026-03-07 in National T20 Cup.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Karachi Region Whites will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Karachi Region Whites.
The outcome corresponding to Tbc A will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Tbc A.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).

question: National T20 Cup: Faisalabad Region vs Tbc A
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Faisalabad Region and Tbc A scheduled for March 9 2026 in National T20 Cup.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Faisalabad Region holds a trader consensus edge in implied probabilities against the yet-to-be-confirmed Pool A opponent (TBC A) in the National T20 Cup knockout clash, driven by their dominant group stage run of three wins in four outings, including a high-scoring chase against Lahore Blues. Recent injury clearance for opener Abdullah Shafique strengthens their top order, while spinners like Ahsan Hafeez exploit Faisalabad's spin-friendly home pitch. Opponent uncertainty tempers odds, but historical data shows Faisalabad's 65% win rate in domestic T20 home games. Key upcoming factors: clear weather forecast aiding batting and Pool A qualifier results potentially pitting them against fatigued Multan or Peshawar sides.

question: National T20 Cup: Faisalabad Region vs Tbc A - Toss Match Double Faisalabad Region Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Faisalabad Region and Tbc A scheduled for 2026-03-09 in National T20 Cup.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Faisalabad Region will be considered correct if  Faisalabad Region is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Tbc A will be considered correct if Tbc A is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

description:

question: Will Russia capture Viroliubivka by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the intersection at 48.608982° N, 37.720231° E in Viroliubivka, Donetsk Oblast, by the specified date 11:59 PM ET.

The intersection will be considered captured if any part of the intersection is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the specified date, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the intersection, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Intersection Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Vir+1.png

Intersection Location in Viroliubivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Vir+2.png

Viroliubivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Vir+3.png

Location on Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Nj4j57W7EW9qUPmRA

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' near-unanimous 98% consensus against Russian forces capturing Viroliubivka by March 31 stems from persistent Ukrainian control of the small Donetsk Oblast village, with no verified advances reported in recent frontline updates. Russian offensives in the nearby Pokrovsk direction have slowed amid heavy casualties, fortified defenses, and harsh winter conditions, as evidenced by geolocated footage and open-source mapping showing static positions. question: Will Trump cut off trade with Spain?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump signs any federal legislation into law or performs any executive action (including executive orders, proclamations, and memoranda) that orders, directs, or enacts a comprehensive ban on imports and exports between the United States and Spain by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

An official announcement, made within this market’s timeframe, from the Trump Administration that a concrete policy to comprehensively ban imports and exports between the US and Spain will definitively be implemented, without such legislation or executive action, will also suffice for a “Yes” resolution. Such an announcement must be tied to a specific policy or set of actions being implemented; mere public statements that a ban will be implemented will not qualify.

A comprehensive ban on imports and exports between the United States and Spain is defined as any trade ban or embargo that applies broadly to imports and exports between the US and Spain. Limited exceptions for certain categories of imports and exports will not disqualify a ban from counting, provided the ban applies generally to US-Spain imports and exports. Bans or embargoes which apply only to a limited subset of U.S.-Spain trade, or tariffs, sanctions, or other punitive trade measures which do not constitute a comprehensive ban on imports or exports between the US and Spain, will not count.

A qualifying ban which is ordered, enacted, or announced within this market’s timeframe will qualify, regardless of when it is scheduled to be implemented or if it is blocked or invalidated prior to implementation.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "No" on President-elect Trump severing trade with Spain, at 99.2% implied probability, driven by the total lack of official announcements, policy signals, or bilateral tensions warranting such disruption. US-Spain trade, valued at over $30 billion annually and embedded in broader US-EU frameworks, faces no active disputes, with Trump's tariff threats focused on China, Mexico, and BRICS nations rather than EU partners. No primary sources indicate plans for targeted decoupling, and presidential trade authorities would require clear justification absent here. Realistic shifts could stem from unexpected escalations, like Spain-aligned EU sanctions provoking retaliation, though inauguration and early 2025 agendas prioritize other priorities.

question: Will T1 qualify to MSI 2026?
description:
The MSI 2026 (Mid-Season Invitational) is scheduled to take place from June 26 to July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

question: Spread: Singapore (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Singapore" if Singapore win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Bangladesh".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Singapore vs. Bangladesh - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 8:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bangladesh enters as heavy favorites against Singapore in their upcoming T20I clash at the Asian Cricket Council Premier Cup, with trader consensus implying over 90% win probability reflecting Bangladesh's superior depth and firepower. Recent developments bolster this sentiment: Bangladesh crushed UAE by 7 wickets in their opener, showcasing explosive batting from Litton Das (75*) and Tanzid Hasan, while Singapore struggled in a narrow 5-wicket win over Thailand amid bowling inconsistencies. Key factors include Bangladesh's spin attack led by Rishad Hossain thriving on Singapore's slow pitches, Singapore's reliance on inexperienced pace, and historical dominance—Bangladesh unbeaten in three prior meetings. Watch for weather disruptions or Singapore's openers Anandakrishnan and Ahmed if chasing an upset.

question: Spread: Bangladesh (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Bangladesh" if Bangladesh win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Singapore".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Singapore vs. Bangladesh - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 8:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bangladesh enters as heavy favorites against Singapore in their upcoming T20I clash at the Asian Cricket Council Premier Cup, with trader consensus implying over 90% win probability reflecting Bangladesh's superior depth and firepower. Recent developments bolster this sentiment: Bangladesh crushed UAE by 7 wickets in their opener, showcasing explosive batting from Litton Das (75*) and Tanzid Hasan, while Singapore struggled in a narrow 5-wicket win over Thailand amid bowling inconsistencies. Key factors include Bangladesh's spin attack led by Rishad Hossain thriving on Singapore's slow pitches, Singapore's reliance on inexperienced pace, and historical dominance—Bangladesh unbeaten in three prior meetings. Watch for weather disruptions or Singapore's openers Anandakrishnan and Ahmed if chasing an upset.

events.title: Germany vs. Ghana - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 30 at 2:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters as heavy favorites in trader consensus against Ghana, reflecting their top-15 FIFA ranking and unbeaten run in recent Nations League matches, including a 5-4 thriller over Hungary. Ghana, languishing outside the top 70, struggles with defensive frailties exposed in AFCON qualifiers, conceding heavily in losses to Niger and Angola. Key factors include Germany's attacking depth led by Musiala and Wirtz, versus Ghana's reliance on aging stars like Ayew amid squad transition. No major injuries reported from official updates, but Ghana's rest disadvantage after midweek travel could widen the gap; historical head-to-head favors Germany 6-1. Odds imply 75%+ win probability, capturing crowd wisdom on form disparity.

events.title: Egypt vs. Spain - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 30 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain holds a commanding edge as trader consensus favorite in the Olympic men's soccer quarterfinal rematch against Egypt at Stade de Lyon, rebounding from a shocking 2-0 group-stage loss to the Pharaohs with a gritty penalty-shootout win over Brazil in the round of 16. Egypt advanced past Paraguay 1-0 on a late Trézéguet goal, leaning on their stout defense led by Emam Ashour, but face a Spain attack firing on all cylinders via Lamine Yamal's wizardry, Fermín López's midfield drive, and Abel Ruiz's finishing. No major injuries reported on official lists; Spain's rest disadvantage is offset by superior U23 talent depth and head-to-head momentum shift, with knockout stakes amplifying underdog upset potential.

events.title: Cyprus vs. Moldova - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 30 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cyprus enters their UEFA Nations League League D matchup against Moldova as the trader-favored side, with market odds implying about a 55-60% win probability driven by strong home form and Moldova's poor away record. Traders note Moldova's four straight away defeats heighten upset risk, yet wisdom of crowds tilts toward the hosts amid even head-to-head history.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vietnam enters as heavy favorites against Bangladesh in their upcoming international friendly, driven by a superior FIFA ranking (113 vs. 183) and strong home-field advantage in Hanoi, where they've won 8 of their last 10. Recent form bolsters trader consensus: Vietnam unbeaten in four straight matches, including a solid draw versus higher-ranked opponents, while Bangladesh has lost seven of eight away games amid defensive frailties. No major injuries reported on official lists, but watch Vietnam's key midfielder Nguyen Quang Hai's fitness after a minor knock. Head-to-head favors Vietnam 5-1 in last six meetings, though Bangladesh's counterattacking style could test over/under goals amid monsoon-season weather risks. Odds reflect ~80% implied win probability for the hosts.

question: Spread: Vietnam (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Vietnam" if Vietnam win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Bangladesh".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: US announces military support of Iran oppostion by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the US government officially announces or confirms that the United States is engaging in military coordination with, or providing military support to, Iranian opposition groups by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For confirmations to qualify, they must be unambiguous, on-the-record public statements issued by an authorized governmental person or entity, speaking in an official capacity.

Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement or qualifying confirmation will not be considered.

“Iranian opposition groups” refers to organized political, paramilitary, insurgent, or resistance groups that publicly oppose the current government of the Islamic Republic of Iran and seek to alter, replace, or overthrow it. This includes groups operating inside or outside Iran that claim to represent opposition to the current governing authorities.

Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.
 
For the purposes of this market, military coordination or support includes but is not limited to joint operations, direct battlefield assistance, intelligence sharing, weapons or equipment transfers, training, logistics support, or other clearly stated military assistance.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 95% implied probability on "No" reflects the complete absence of official US statements or actions signaling military support for Iranian opposition groups by March 31. US policy under the Biden administration prioritizes sanctions on Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, diplomatic isolation over its nuclear activities, and defensive aid to Israel amid regional proxy conflicts, without endorsing armed dissidents. Recent State Department rhetoric has reiterated moral support for Iranian protesters following crackdowns, but no commitments to matériel or operations have emerged. Ongoing US focus on Ukraine aid and Houthi threats further diminishes prospects for this escalation, aligning trader consensus with historical restraint in direct intervention.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (HIGH) $100 by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 25% implied probability for Crude Oil (CL) hitting $90 by June's end, anchored by current WTI futures at $81.50/bbl amid persistent supply overhang—EIA reported a 5.8MM barrel inventory build last week, exceeding expectations—and softening demand signals from China's tepid industrial output. Bearish trader consensus weighs OPEC+'s 2.2MM bpd voluntary cuts against 5MM bpd spare capacity, plus recession fears curbing consumption. Critical catalyst ahead: June 26 EIA storage report, where a surprise draw could lift odds; resolution ties to June 28 NYMEX settle above the threshold, underscoring volatility from geopolitics like Red Sea disruptions.

question: Will a team from LCK (South Korea) win MSI 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the region or country of the esports team winning 1st place in the Mid-Season Invitational 2026 (MSI 2026), currently scheduled for June 26 - July 12, 2026.

If the winner of MSI 2026 is not determined by July 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

Regions counted in MSI 2026:
- LCK (South Korea)
- LPL (China)
- LEC (Europe / EMEA)
- LCP (Asia-Pacific)
- LCS (North America)
- CBLOL (Brazil)

events.title: LoL: MSI 2026 Winning Region
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the region or country of the esports team winning 1st place in the Mid-Season Invitational 2026 (MSI 2026), currently scheduled for June 26 - July 12, 2026.

Regions counted in MSI 2026:
- LCK (South Korea)
- LPL (China)
- LEC (Europe / EMEA)
- LCP (Asia-Pacific)
- LCS (North America)
- CBLOL (Brazil)

eventMetadata.context_description: LCK's commanding 69.5% implied probability stems from South Korean teams' recent international dominance, highlighted by Gen.G's MSI 2024 victory over LPL's BLG and T1's thrilling Worlds 2024 grand final win against the same opponent, reinforcing Korea's edge in high-pressure playoffs and macro play. LPL holds steady at 22% as China's deep talent pool produced consistent finalists, though vulnerabilities in late-game execution persist. LEC's 8.2% reflects G2's semifinal run at Worlds but broader European inconsistencies, while LCS, CBLOL, and LCP trail due to poor showings on the global stage and weaker regional splits. Traders weigh these trends against upcoming 2025 splits for sustained momentum.

question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (HIGH) $150 by end of June?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a modest 25% implied probability for Crude Oil (CL) hitting $90 by June's end, anchored by current WTI futures at $81.50/bbl amid persistent supply overhang—EIA reported a 5.8MM barrel inventory build last week, exceeding expectations—and softening demand signals from China's tepid industrial output. Bearish trader consensus weighs OPEC+'s 2.2MM bpd voluntary cuts against 5MM bpd spare capacity, plus recession fears curbing consumption. Critical catalyst ahead: June 26 EIA storage report, where a surprise draw could lift odds; resolution ties to June 28 NYMEX settle above the threshold, underscoring volatility from geopolitics like Red Sea disruptions.

question: Will Faker win a Finals MVP Award at an international event in 2026?
description:
The market will resolve to "Yes" if Lee "Faker" Sang-hyeok is officially announced as the Finals MVP of any international event in 2026 (such as First Stand, MSI, Esports World Cup, Worlds, or any other officially recognized global Riot Games LoL tournament). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market may resolve to “No” when it becomes impossible for Faker to win such an award (e.g., if his team is eliminated from all remaining international events without him receiving an MVP, or the season concludes without qualification).

This market will resolve to “No” if no Finals MVP Award has been officially announced for Faker by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official LoL Esports channels and Riot Games (https://lolesports.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Despite T1's Worlds 2024 triumph where Faker earned FMVP and posted elite KDA splits, historical longevity data shows sharp declines post-28 for ADCs and mids, compounded by his past wrist injuries requiring surgery. T1's core with Gumayusi and Keria remains potent, but roster flux, grueling LCK schedule, and surging teams like Gen.G tilt trader consensus toward regression over repeat heroics.

question: TJR mentee Timmeh earns prop payout in 30 days?
description:
This market resolves to “Yes” if Timmeh successfully completes the proprietary trading 30 day challenge as outlined on stream earning a payout from a proprietary trading firm. Otherwise, this market resolves to “No.”

“Successfully completes the challenge” refers to satisfying the stated profit target and all risk, drawdown, and trading requirements necessary to qualify for a profit payout under the rules described during the stream.

A qualifying payout must be directly attributable to performance achieved during the 30-day challenge period.

If Timmeh does not successfully complete the challenge within the 30-day window, this market resolves to “No.”

The primary resolution source will be the livestream itself, including on-stream confirmation and evidence of challenge completion.

question: Will there be a US x Iran ceasefire before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Kevin Warsh being confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Fed Chair, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market are official information from the U.S. Senate and official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached or a consensus of credible reporting that Warsh has been confirmed will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on events preceding Kevin Warsh's confirmation hinges on President-elect Trump's failure to nominate him for any role amid rapid cabinet announcements, including Scott Bessent for Treasury Secretary on November 22. Earlier speculation positioned Warsh as a top contender for Treasury or future Fed Chair, but current pricing reflects trader consensus on prolonged delays absent formal nomination. Senate confirmation processes ramp up after January 20 inauguration, with hearings potentially exposing partisan hurdles. Upcoming nominee reveals and Federal Reserve dynamics, including Jerome Powell's May 2026 term end, represent key catalysts that could alter probabilities.

question: Will the Fed cut rates before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point before Kevin Warsh is confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Kevin Warsh being confirmed as chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of Warsh as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

Both rate cuts resulting from scheduled Federal Open Market Committee meetings and emergency rate cuts will qualify as decreasing the upper bound of the target federal funds rate.

The primary resolution sources for this market are official information from the U.S. Senate and the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm). However, consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Russia enter Novyi Donbas by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Novyi Donbas, Donetsk Oblast, (48.450718° N, 37.181238° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

events.title: Will Russia enter Novyi Donbas by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Novyi Donbas, Donetsk Oblast, (48.450718° N, 37.181238° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have made incremental gains in Donetsk Oblast, capturing villages near Novyi Donbas amid intensified assaults on the Pokrovsk axis, but have not yet entered the settlement according to frontline reports from the Institute for the Study of War. Ukrainian defenses, bolstered by recent Western aid including artillery shells, continue to inflict heavy casualties on advancing Russian units, slowing momentum despite Moscow's numerical superiority. Trader consensus reflects uncertainty over Russia's ability to breach fortified positions before the market deadline, with odds incorporating base rates from prior slow advances like those around Avdiivka. Upcoming events include potential U.S. aid packages and winter weather impacts, which could further constrain operations.

question: AWS service disrupted by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Amazon Web services experiences any service interruption event with a severity classification of “disrupted” by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The severity classification of an AWS service interruption event may be found on the AWS Health Dashboard (https://health.aws.amazon.com/health/status) when the relevant event is selected under “List of events.” Only publicly visible service events listed on the AWS Health Dashboard status page qualify. Account-specific AWS Health events do not count.

Qualifying incidents may include outages and other issues whose severity AWS classifies as “disrupted.” 

This market will resolve as soon as the severity of any service interruption is classified as “disrupted”, regardless of subsequent revisions or corrections. Revisions to the severity classification of any event to a classification of “disrupted” will be considered as long as those revisions are published within this market’s timeframe.

If an incident is ongoing at this market’s resolution time, the market may remain open until that incident receives an official severity classification, and it will resolve based on the first such classification published.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Amazon Web Services Health Dashboard (https://health.aws.amazon.com/health/status).

A brief US-East-1 networking blip on February 28 was contained within hours, without broader impact, and March has remained quiet amid routine maintenance. Traders monitor status.aws.amazon.com and DownDetector spikes, with resolution hinging on confirmed multi-region impairments lasting over an hour.

question: Will FaZe win a Tier 1 event in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if FaZe Clan's CS2 roster wins (takes 1st place in) any Tier 1 CS2 tournament that concludes by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "Tier 1 Event" refers to any tournament classified as S-Tier on Liquipedia.net. Online-only events, or events downgraded prior to event start (e.g., downgraded to A-Tier due to team attendance) do not qualify. If a Tier 1 event is downgraded after it has concluded, this event will still qualify as a "Tier 1 Event". 

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the tournament organizers (e.g., ESL, PGL, BLAST.tv, etc.).

Recent form shows top-4 finishes but repeated semifinal exits against Vitality, G2, and MOUZ, hampered by inconsistent clutches and anti-eco rounds despite roski's standout aim and karrigan's veteran leadership. Intensifying competition from resurgent European superteams, coupled with no major roster overhauls, leaves little momentum for a breakthrough amid a packed 2026 calendar of 10+ Tier 1s where upsets favor the wisdom of crowds pricing in their struggles.

question: Will SOFR hit 3.80% (High) between March 1 and March 31?
description:
The Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) is a measure of the cost of borrowing cash overnight, collateralized by US Treasury securities. SOFR is used to price loans, bonds, derivatives, and more. The SOFR rate is published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York each business day at 8:00 AM ET, for the previous business day.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Federal Reserve Bank of New York publishes a daily SOFR rate (%) equal to or above the listed value for any business day between March 1 and March 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the SOFR rate is equal to or above the listed value during the specified period, or when SOFR data is published for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026. If no data is published for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026 by April 07, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data published up to that point. Business day refers to any day treated as such by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York.

Any revisions to the daily SOFR values published by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York prior to the release of SOFR data for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026 will be considered; however, these revisions will not disqualify a previously published SOFR rate from counting. Any revisions published after the release of SOFR data for the final business day on or before March 31, 2026 will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Federal Reserve Bank of New York, specifically the Secured Overnight Financing Rate Data table at https://www.newyorkfed.org/markets/reference-rates/sofr.

Note: This market’s resolution source publishes the SOFR rate to two decimal places (e.g. 3.65%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market.

question: Will a 1 seed win the 2026 Men's NCAA Tournament?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the seed number of the 2026 Men's Basketball NCAA Tournament champion equals the listed number. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no champion declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a top seed claiming the NCAA Tournament national championship, with the #1 seed at 48% implied probability driven by UConn, Purdue, and Houston advancing to the Elite Eight alongside historical dominance—#1 seeds have reached the Final Four in six of the last 10 tournaments. Illinois' #3 seed surge to 35% reflects its gritty wins over Duquesne and Washington State, setting up a marquee Midwest regional final against Purdue. Clustered odds around #2-#7 seeds (33-34.5%) underscore bracket strength from Duke (#4), Clemson (#6), and others amid minimal injury disruptions. NC State's Cinderella run past Texas Tech (#6), Marquette (#2), and into South regional final elevates #11 to 24%, though top seeds hold schedule and talent edges heading into regional finals.

This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 Naismith Defensive Player of the Year award for the 2026 Men’s NCAA Division 1 Basketball season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by NCAA rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA season is cancelled, postponed after April 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Naismith Defensive Player of the Year market shows razor-thin margins atop a crowded field of freshmen big men, with Flory Bidunga's 45% implied probability barely ahead of Javontae Campbell, Rueben Chinyelu, Somto Cyril, and Zuby Ejiofor at 44.5%. This parity stems from elite early-season block rates—Bidunga averaging 3.3 per game for Kansas, Cyril swatting 3.0 at Georgia, and peers like Chinyelu and Ejiofor posting comparable per-40-minute defensive outputs amid strong team starts. Small sample sizes, shared freshman hype from recruiting ranks, and balanced steals/rebounding contributions prevent separation, mirroring past races where tournament dominance later clarifies standouts.

question: Will US Catanzaro 1929 win on 2026-03-17?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 17, 2026
If US Catanzaro 1929 wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: US Catanzaro 1929 vs. Modena FC 2018
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Serie B game, scheduled for Tuesday, March 17, 2026 between US Catanzaro 1929 and Modena FC 2018.

eventMetadata.context_description: Modena FC 2018 holds a slim edge in trader consensus at 36% implied probability, driven by their stronger Serie C Girone B standing (4th with 20 points from 10 matches) and recent form including three wins in five, contrasting Catanzaro's middling 10th place (13 points) marred by four losses in six. However, US Catanzaro 1929's home advantage at Stadio Nicola Ceravolo—where they've earned 10 of 13 points—combined with a tight head-to-head history (Modena's lone recent win was 2-1 away) fuels the razor-thin 33.5% backing, while draw pricing at 30.5% reflects frequent stalemates in their matchups and both sides' defensive resilience amid no major reported injuries or suspensions. Momentum tilts Modena, but Catanzaro's urgency in the playoff chase keeps this contest fiercely competitive.

question: Spread: SC Braga (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Primeira Liga game, scheduled for March 22 at 4:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "SC Braga" if SC Braga win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Porto".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaportugal.pt. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Braga vs. FC Porto - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primeira Liga game, scheduled for March 22 at 4:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Porto enters as the clear trader favorite against SC Braga, with market odds reflecting their historical dominance—winning 7 of the last 10 head-to-heads—and current Primeira Liga momentum, sitting atop the standings with just one loss in 12 matches. Braga's solid home form at Estádio Municipal (unbeaten in five) adds upset potential, but Porto's defensive solidity (league-best 5 goals conceded) and attacking firepower from Samu Omorodion loom large. No major injuries reported on official lists, though Braga monitors Ricardo Horta's fitness; Porto benefits from a rest edge post-midweek. Rivalry intensity and title implications could fuel a tight contest, where trader sentiment hinges on Porto's away execution.
